grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: too for agreeing
grammarPoints.rule: Use do/does + too to agree with a positive statement. Use don't/doesn't when you do not agree.
grammarPoints.pattern: subject + do/does + too  or  subject + don't/doesn't

intro_examples.text: I ride my bike to school. I do, too.
intro_examples.jp: 私は自転車で学校に行きます。私もそうです。

intro_examples.text: Eva takes the bus. Her friend doesn't. She drives.
intro_examples.jp: エヴァはバスに乗ります。彼女の友達はそうではありません（バスに乗りません）。彼女は車を運転します。

table.title: Agreeing and Disagreeing
columns_jp: 賛成・同意する（私もそうです）
反対・同意しない（私は違います）

cells: I ride my bike to school.

notes: Use 'do/does + too' only when agreeing with a positive statement.
don't = do not, doesn't = does not

tab1_samples.text: I ride my scooter to school. I do, too.
tab1_samples.jp: 私はスクーターで学校に行きます。私もそうです。

tab1_samples.text: I take the bus to school. I don't. I take the subway.
tab1_samples.jp: 私はバスで学校に行きます。私は違います。地下鉄に乗ります。

tab1_samples.text: My brother rides his bike to school. My brother does, too.
tab1_samples.jp: 私の兄は自転車で学校に行きます。私の兄もそうです。

tab1_samples.text: My sister rides her skateboard to school. My sister doesn't. She walks.
tab1_samples.jp: 私の姉はスケートボードで学校に行きます。私の姉は違います。彼女は歩きます。

tab1_samples.text: I want to take the ferry. I do, too.
tab1_samples.jp: 私はフェリーに乗りたいです。私もそうです。

tab1_samples.text: I go fast on my bike. I don't.
tab1_samples.jp: 私は自転車で速く走ります。私は違います。

tab1_samples.text: I ride a scooter. I do, too.
tab1_samples.jp: 私はスクーターに乗ります。私もそうです。

tab1_samples.text: My mom drives me to school. My mom doesn't.
tab1_samples.jp: お母さんは私を車で学校に送ってくれます。私のお母さんは違います。

tab1_samples.text: My dad walks a lot. My dad does, too.
tab1_samples.jp: お父さんはたくさん歩きます。私のお父さんもそうです。

tab1_samples.text: Rodrigo rides his bike to the bakery. His brother does, too.
tab1_samples.jp: ロドリゴは自転車でパン屋に行きます。彼の弟もそうです。

rules.title: Use do, too / does, too for positive agreement
rules.jp_title: 肯定文への同意には do, too / does, too を使う
rules.subtitle: Use 'do, too' for I/you/we/they. Use 'does, too' for he/she/it or singular subjects.
rules.jp_subtitle: I/you/we/theyには「do, too」を、he/she/itや単数の主語には「does, too」を使って同意を表します。

transforms.from: I want to take the ferry. I does, too.
transforms.to: I want to take the ferry. I do, too.

transforms.from: George rides his bike. His brother do, too.
transforms.to: George rides his bike. His brother does, too.

examples.text: I want to take the ferry. I do, too.
examples.jp: 私はフェリーに乗りたいです。私もそうです。

examples.text: George rides his bike. His brother does, too.
examples.jp: ジョージは自転車に乗ります。彼の弟もそうです。

rules.title: Use don't / doesn't for disagreement
rules.jp_title: 同意しないときは don't / doesn't を使う
rules.subtitle: Use 'don't' or 'doesn't' to show that you or another person does not do the action.
rules.jp_subtitle: 自分や他の人がその行動をしない（同意しない）ことを示すには、「don't」や「doesn't」を使います。

transforms.from: I ride a scooter. I doesn't.
transforms.to: I ride a scooter. I don't.

transforms.from: My mom drives me to school. My mom don't.
transforms.to: My mom drives me to school. My mom doesn't.

examples.text: I ride a scooter. I don't.
examples.jp: 私はスクーターに乗ります。私は違います。

examples.text: My mom drives me to school. My mom doesn't.
examples.jp: お母さんは私を車で学校に送ってくれます。私のお母さんは違います。

rules.title: Provide explanations when disagreeing
rules.jp_title: 同意しないときは、説明を加えるのが自然
rules.subtitle: When you disagree, it's typical to give a short explanation of what actually happens.
rules.jp_subtitle: 反対するときは、実際にどうするかの短い説明を付け加えるのが一般的です。

transforms.from: I take the bus. I don't.
transforms.to: I take the bus. I don't. I take the subway.

transforms.from: Paula rides her bike. George doesn't.
transforms.to: Paula rides her bike. George doesn't. He rides his scooter.

examples.text: I take the bus. I don't. I take the subway.
examples.jp: 私はバスに乗ります。私は違います。地下鉄に乗ります。

examples.text: Paula rides her bike. George doesn't. He rides his scooter.
examples.jp: ポーラは自転車に乗ります。ジョージは違います。彼はスクーターに乗ります。

rules.title: Watch out for be-verbs
rules.jp_title: be動詞の文には be動詞を使って同意する
rules.subtitle: If the statement has a form of 'be', do not use do/does. Use 'am, too' or 'is/are, too'.
rules.jp_subtitle: 元の文がbe動詞の場合、do/doesは使えません。「am, too」や「is/are, too」を使います。

transforms.from: They are from Chile. I do, too.
transforms.to: They are from Chile. I am, too.

transforms.from: I am happy. My brother does, too.
transforms.to: I am happy. My brother is, too.

examples.text: They are from Chile. I am, too.
examples.jp: 彼らはチリ出身です。私もそうです。

examples.text: I am happy. My brother is, too.
examples.jp: 私は幸せです。私の弟もそうです。

mixed_samples.text: I want to take the ferry. I do, too.
mixed_samples.jp: 私はフェリーに乗りたいです。私もそうです。

mixed_samples.text: George rides his bike. His brother does, too.
mixed_samples.jp: ジョージは自転車に乗ります。彼の弟もそうです。

mixed_samples.text: I ride a scooter. I don't.
mixed_samples.jp: 私はスクーターに乗ります。私は違います。

mixed_samples.text: My mom drives me to school. My mom doesn't.
mixed_samples.jp: お母さんは私を車で学校に送ってくれます。私のお母さんは違います。

mixed_samples.text: I take the bus. I don't. I take the subway.
mixed_samples.jp: 私はバスに乗ります。私は違います。地下鉄に乗ります。

mixed_samples.text: Paula rides her bike. George doesn't. He rides his scooter.
mixed_samples.jp: ポーラは自転車に乗ります。ジョージは違います。彼はスクーターに乗ります。

mixed_samples.text: They are from Chile. I am, too.
mixed_samples.jp: 彼らはチリ出身です。私もそうです。

mixed_samples.text: I am happy. My brother is, too.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は幸せです。私の弟もそうです。

mixed_samples.text: His mother takes a taxi to work. His father doesn't. He takes a bus.
mixed_samples.jp: 彼の母親はタクシーで仕事に行きます。彼の父親は違います。彼はバスに乗ります。

mixed_samples.text: My sister rides her scooter to school. I don't. I walk.
mixed_samples.jp: 私の姉はスクーターで学校に行きます。私は違います。歩きます。

stem: I ride my scooter to school. I

explanation.title: First Person Agreement
explanation.body: For 'I', we use 'do' to agree with a positive statement: 'I do, too.'

tab3_quiz.jp: 私はスクーターで学校に行きます。私もそうです。

stem: My brother rides his bike to school. My brother

explanation.title: Third Person Singular Agreement
explanation.body: 'My brother' is singular (he), so we use 'does' to agree: 'My brother does, too.'

tab3_quiz.jp: 私の兄は自転車で学校に行きます。私の兄もそうです。

stem: I take the bus to school. I

explanation.title: Disagreeing (First Person)
explanation.body: To say you do NOT do the action, use 'don't' for 'I': 'I don't.'

tab3_quiz.jp: 私はバスで学校に行きます。私は違います。地下鉄に乗ります。

stem: My sister rides her skateboard to school. My sister

explanation.title: Disagreeing (Third Person Singular)
explanation.body: 'My sister' is singular (she), so we use 'doesn't' to disagree: 'My sister doesn't.'

tab3_quiz.jp: 私の姉はスケートボードで学校に行きます。私の姉は違います。彼女は歩きます。

stem: I want to take the ferry. I

explanation.title: Agreeing with action verb
explanation.body: 'want' is an action verb, so we use 'do' (for I) to agree.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私はフェリーに乗りたいです。私もそうです。

stem: My mom drives me to school. My mom

explanation.title: Disagreeing third person
explanation.body: 'My mom' is singular, so we use 'doesn't' to say she does not drive.

tab3_quiz.jp: お母さんは私を車で学校に送ってくれます。私のお母さんは違います。

stem: Rodrigo rides his bike. His brother

explanation.title: Agreeing third person
explanation.body: 'His brother' is singular, so we agree using 'does, too'.

tab3_quiz.jp: ロドリゴは自転車に乗ります。彼の弟もそうです。

explanation.title: Agreeing with Be-verb
explanation.body: The statement uses 'are' (be-verb), so we must agree with a form of be: 'I am, too.'

tab3_quiz.jp: 彼らはチリ出身です。私もそうです。

stem: I take a taxi to the museum. George

explanation.title: Action Verb Agreement
explanation.body: 'George' is singular, so he takes 'does' to agree: 'George does, too.'

tab3_quiz.jp: 私はタクシーで美術館に行きます。ジョージもそうです。ポーラは違います。

explanation.title: Action Verb Disagreement
explanation.body: Use 'don't' to disagree with a statement using an action verb when the subject is 'I'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私はスクーターに乗ります。私は違います。

stem:
A: 'I take the subway to school.'
B: '___________'

explanation.title: Action Verb Agreement
explanation.body: 'take' is an action verb and the subject is 'I', so 'I do, too' is correct.

tab4_master.jp: どの返答が正しい同意を表していますか？

stem:
A: 'My father rides a motorcycle.'
B: 'My father _________, too.'

explanation.title: Third Person Singular
explanation.body: 'My father' is singular, so we agree using 'does, too'.

tab4_master.jp: どの返答が正しいですか？

stem: Find the incorrect response for agreement.

explanation.title: Be-verb Agreement
explanation.body: Because 'are' is a form of 'be', we cannot use 'do, too'. We must use a form of be, like 'am, too'.

tab4_master.jp: 同意の返答として正しくないものを選んでください。

stem:
A: 'My mother drives a car.'
B: 'My mother _________.'

explanation.title: Disagreeing third person
explanation.body: 'My mother' is singular, so we disagree with 'doesn't'.

tab4_master.jp: 反対・同意しない文を完成させてください。

answers: I ride my scooter, and Maya does, too.
I ride my scooter, and Maya do, too.
I ride my scooter, and Maya is, too.
I ride my scooter, and Maya doesn't, too.

explanation.title: Third Person Singular Action
explanation.body: 'Maya' is third person singular, so 'does, too' is correct.

tab4_master.jp: どの文が正しいですか？

tab4_master.cue: Build: I want to take the ferry. I do, too.
tab4_master.jp: 私はフェリーに乗りたいです。私もそうです。

tab4_master.cue: Build: My dad walks. My dad does, too.
tab4_master.jp: お父さんは歩きます。私のお父さんもそうです。

tab4_master.cue: Build: I ride a scooter. I don't.
tab4_master.jp: 私はスクーターに乗ります。私は違います。

tab4_master.cue: Build: Paula rides her bike. George doesn't.
tab4_master.jp: ポーラは自転車に乗ります。ジョージは違います。

tab4_master.cue: Build: They are happy. I am, too.
tab4_master.jp: 彼らは幸せです。私もそうです。

japanese.title: 同意を表す too
japanese.rule: 相手の肯定文に同意するときは「do/does + too」を使います。同意しない（自分は違う）ときは「don't/doesn't」を使います。
japanese.pattern: 主語 + do/does + too  または  主語 + don't/doesn't

examples.sentence: I ride my scooter to school. I do, too.
I take the bus to school. I don't. I take the subway.
My brother rides his bike to school. My brother does, too.
My sister rides her skateboard to school. My sister doesn't. She walks.
I want to take the ferry. I do, too.
I go fast on my bike. I don't.
I ride a scooter. I do, too.
My mom drives me to school. My mom doesn't.
My dad walks a lot. My dad does, too.
Rodrigo rides his bike to the bakery. His brother does, too.
George rides his bike. His brother does, too.
I ride a scooter. I don't.
I take the bus. I don't. I take the subway.
Paula rides her bike. George doesn't. He rides his scooter.
They are from Chile. I am, too.
I am happy. My brother is, too.
His mother takes a taxi to work. His father doesn't. He takes a bus.
My sister rides her scooter to school. I don't. I walk.

source.note: Workbook page 31 and Grammar Workbook pages 10-11 used for agreement logic.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: but as a contrast
grammarPoints.rule: Use 'but' to connect two ideas that show contrast or difference.

intro_examples.text: My mother takes the bus to work, but my father takes the subway.
intro_examples.jp: お母さんはバスで仕事に行きますが、お父さんは地下鉄で行きます。

table.title: Connecting with but
columns_jp: 2つ目の反対・対比する考え
notes: Use a comma (,) before 'but' when connecting two complete sentences.
But shows how two things are different.

tab1_samples.text: My sister walks to school, but my brother rides his bike to school.
tab1_samples.jp: 私の姉は歩いて学校に行きますが、私の兄は自転車で学校に行きます。

tab1_samples.text: I don't like to pedal uphill, but I like to coast downhill.
tab1_samples.jp: 私は坂をペダルでこぎ上るのは好きではありませんが、坂をペダルをこがずに下るのは好きです。

tab1_samples.text: She walks to the movie theater, but she takes a bus to the mall.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼女は映画館まで歩きますが、モールまではバスに乗ります。

tab1_samples.text: He eats snacks after school, but he always eats his dinner.
tab1_samples.jp: 彼は放課後におやつを食べますが、いつも夕食はしっかり食べます。

tab1_samples.text: A penguin can swim, but it can't fly.
tab1_samples.jp: ペンギンは泳ぐことができますが、飛ぶことはできません。

tab1_samples.text: The girl is smiling, but the boy is frowning.
tab1_samples.jp: 女の子は微笑んでいますが、男の子は顔をしかめています。

tab1_samples.text: My mother likes bread, but my father likes meat.
tab1_samples.jp: お母さんはパンが好きですが、お父さんはお肉が好きです。

tab1_samples.text: The boy rides his scooter to school, but the girl rides her bike to school.
tab1_samples.jp: 男の子はスクーターで学校に行きますが、女の子は自転車で学校に行きます。

tab1_samples.text: The girl eats breakfast at eight o'clock, but the boy eats breakfast at seven thirty.
tab1_samples.jp: 女の子は8時に朝食を食べますが、男の子は7時半に朝食を食べます。

tab1_samples.text: The boy has a pet rabbit, but the girl has a pet dog.
tab1_samples.jp: 男の子はペットのウサギを飼っていますが、女の子はペットの犬を飼っています。

rules.title: Use but to show differences
rules.jp_title: 違いを示すために but を使う
rules.subtitle: Use 'but' to join two clauses that have opposite or different ideas.
rules.jp_subtitle: 反対の考えや異なる考えを持つ2つの節を結合するために「but」を使います。

transforms.from: My bike is small. My bike is fast.
transforms.to: My bike is small but fast.

transforms.from: I ride it during the day. I don't ride it at night.
transforms.to: I ride it during the day, but not at night.

examples.text: My bike is small but fast.
examples.jp: 私の自転車は小さいですが速いです。

examples.text: I ride my bike to school every day, but I don't ride it on Sundays.
examples.jp: 私は毎日自転車で学校に行きますが、日曜日には乗りません。

rules.title: Use comma before but for complete sentences
rules.jp_title: 完全な文をつなぐときは but の前にカンマを置く
rules.subtitle: When connecting two independent clauses (complete sentences), put a comma before 'but'.
rules.jp_subtitle: 2つの独立した節（それぞれが完全な文）をつなぐときは、「but」の前にカンマを置きます。

transforms.from: The train is fast but the bus is slow.
transforms.to: The train is fast, but the bus is slow.

transforms.from: We like reading but they like watching TV.
transforms.to: We like reading, but they like watching TV.

examples.text: The train is fast, but the bus is slow.
examples.jp: 電車は速いですが、バスは遅いです。

examples.text: We like reading, but they like watching TV.
examples.jp: 私たちは読書が好きですが、彼らはテレビを見るのが好きです。

rules.title: Contrasting with can and can't
rules.jp_title: can と can't の対比
rules.subtitle: Use 'but' to show what an animal or person can and cannot do.
rules.jp_subtitle: 動物や人が「できること」と「できないこと」を示すときにも「but」が便利です。

transforms.from: A helicopter can fly. It can't sail.
transforms.to: A helicopter can fly, but it can't sail.

transforms.from: I can walk to the school. My brother can't.
transforms.to: I can walk to the school, but my brother can't.

examples.text: A helicopter can fly, but it can't sail.
examples.jp: ヘリコプターは飛べますが、航海はできません。

examples.text: I can walk to the school, but my brother can't.
examples.jp: 私は学校まで歩けますが、兄は歩けません。

rules.title: Contrasting opposites
rules.jp_title: 反対のことを対比させる
rules.subtitle: Use 'but' to link opposite adjectives or states.
rules.jp_subtitle: 反対の形容詞や状態を結びつけるために「but」を使います。

transforms.from: I am young. My grandfather is old.
transforms.to: I am young, but my grandfather is old.

transforms.from: My scooter has two wheels. Our car has four wheels.
transforms.to: My scooter has two wheels, but our car has four wheels.

examples.text: I am young, but my grandfather is old.
examples.jp: 私は若いですが、祖父は年をとっています。

examples.text: My scooter has two wheels, but our car has four wheels.
examples.jp: 私のスクーターは2輪ですが、うちの車は4輪です。

mixed_samples.text: My bike is small but fast.
mixed_samples.jp: 私の自転車は小さいですが速いです。

mixed_samples.text: I ride my bike to school every day, but I don't ride it on Sundays.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は毎日自転車で学校に行きますが、日曜日には乗りません。

mixed_samples.text: The train is fast, but the bus is slow.
mixed_samples.jp: 電車は速いですが、バスは遅いです。

mixed_samples.text: We like reading, but they like watching TV.
mixed_samples.jp: 私たちは読書が好きですが、彼らはテレビを見るのが好きです。

mixed_samples.text: A helicopter can fly, but it can't sail.
mixed_samples.jp: ヘリコプターは飛べますが、航海はできません。

mixed_samples.text: I can walk to the school, but my brother can't.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は学校まで歩けますが、兄は歩けません。

mixed_samples.text: I am young, but my grandfather is old.
mixed_samples.jp: 私は若いですが、祖父は年をとっています。

mixed_samples.text: My scooter has two wheels, but our car has four wheels.
mixed_samples.jp: 私のスクーターは2輪ですが、うちの車は4輪です。

mixed_samples.text: My dad takes the subway, but my mom takes the bus.
mixed_samples.jp: お父さんは地下鉄に乗りますが、お母さんはバスに乗ります。

mixed_samples.text: A ferry goes on water, but a helicopter goes in the air.
mixed_samples.jp: フェリーは水上を行きますが、ヘリコプターは空中を行きます。

explanation.title: Conjunction for Contrast
explanation.body: The sentences show different ways of getting to school, so we use 'but' to show contrast.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私の姉は歩いて学校に行きますが、私の兄は自転車で行きます。

explanation.title: Agreement Conjunction
explanation.body: Since both have red scooters, there is no contrast. We use 'and' (or simply agree with 'too').

tab3_quiz.jp: 私は赤いスクーターを持っていて、ジョンもそれを持っています。

explanation.title: Contrasting Adjectives
explanation.body: 'fast' and 'slow' are opposites, so we use 'but' to connect them.

tab3_quiz.jp: 電車は速いですが、バスは遅いです。

explanation.title: Can and Can't Contrast
explanation.body: 'can swim' contrasts with 'can't fly', so we use 'but'.

tab3_quiz.jp: ペンギンは泳ぐことができますが、飛ぶことはできません。

explanation.title: Contrasting States
explanation.body: 'young' and 'old' contrast, so 'but' is the correct connector.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私は若いですが、祖父は年をとっています。

explanation.title: Adding Similar Ideas
explanation.body: Both actions are the same, so there is no contrast. Use 'and'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私の姉は歩きますし、私の兄も歩きます。

stem: I want to fly in a helicopter,

explanation.title: Desire vs Reality
explanation.body: Wanting to do something but not being able to is a contrast, so we use 'but'.

tab3_quiz.jp: ヘリコプターで飛びたいですが、お金がありません。

explanation.title: Contrasting Transportation
explanation.body: Comparing different modes of travel for two people requires 'but'.

tab3_quiz.jp: お父さんは地下鉄に乗りますが、お母さんはバスに乗ります。

stem: a helicopter goes in the air.

explanation.title: Different Mediums
explanation.body: 'on water' and 'in the air' are different places of travel, so we use 'but'.

tab3_quiz.jp: フェリーは水上を行きますが、ヘリコプターは空中を行きます。

explanation.title: Different Hobbies
explanation.body: 'reading' contrasts with 'watching TV', so we use 'but'.

tab3_quiz.jp: 私は読書が好きですが、彼らはテレビを見るのが好きです。

stem: Which sentence is written correctly with a comma?
answers: My sister walks to school, but my brother rides his bike.
My sister walks to school but my brother rides his bike.
My sister walks to school, and my brother rides his bike, too.
My sister walks to school but, my brother rides his bike.

explanation.title: Punctuation with But
explanation.body: Put a comma before 'but' when joining two complete sentences.

tab4_master.jp: カンマが正しく使われている文はどれですか？

stem: Complete: 'The Eastern snake-necked turtle smells terrible, _________ apple trees smell great.'

explanation.title: Contrast with stinky and good smells
explanation.body: 'smells terrible' contrasts with 'smells great', so we use 'but'.

tab4_master.jp: 完成させてください：クビナガガメはひどい臭いがしますが、リンゴの木は素晴らしい香りがします。

stem: Which sentence correctly shows a contrast?
answers: The train is fast, but the bus is slow.
The train is fast, and the train is long.
The train is fast, or the bus is fast.
The train is fast because it has an engine.

explanation.title: Contrasting Adjectives
explanation.body: Fast vs slow is a clear difference, connected by 'but'.

tab4_master.jp: 対比を表す正しい文はどれですか？

stem: Complete: 'I have an amazing bike. It can fly, _________ it can't swim.'

explanation.title: Can vs Can't
explanation.body: We use 'but' to show a contrast between what it can do ('fly') and cannot do ('swim').

tab4_master.jp: 完成させてください：私は素晴らしい自転車を持っています。それは飛べますが、泳げません。

answers: Sandra rides her bike at the park, but I walk my dog.
Sandra rides her bike at the park, but I walk my dog, too.
Sandra rides her bike at the park, and I walk my dog.
Sandra rides her bike at the park but, I walk my dog.

explanation.title: Punctuation with But
explanation.body: Sandra's riding contrasts with my walking, so 'Sandra rides... , but I walk...' is correct.

tab4_master.jp: 正しい文を選んでください。

tab4_master.cue: Build: My sister walks to school, but my brother rides his bike.
tab4_master.jp: 私の姉は歩いて学校に行きますが、私の兄は自転車で行きます。

tab4_master.cue: Build: A penguin can swim, but it can't fly.
tab4_master.jp: ペンギンは泳ぐことができますが、飛ぶことはできません。

tab4_master.cue: Build: The train is fast, but the bus is slow.
tab4_master.jp: 電車は速いですが、バスは遅いです。

tab4_master.cue: Build: I am young, but my grandfather is old.
tab4_master.jp: 私は若いですが、祖父は年をとっています。

tab4_master.cue: Build: My bike is small but fast.
tab4_master.jp: 私の自転車は小さいですが速いです。

japanese.title: 対比の but
japanese.rule: 「but」を使って、2つの異なる考えや対比する事柄をつなぎます。2つの完全な文をつなぐときは、「but」の前にカンマを置きます。

examples.sentence: My sister walks to school, but my brother rides his bike to school.
I don't like to pedal uphill, but I like to coast downhill.
She walks to the movie theater, but she takes a bus to the mall.
He eats snacks after school, but he always eats his dinner.
A penguin can swim, but it can't fly.
The girl is smiling, but the boy is frowning.
My mother likes bread, but my father likes meat.
The boy rides his scooter to school, but the girl rides her bike to school.
The girl eats breakfast at eight o'clock, but the boy eats breakfast at seven thirty.
The boy has a pet rabbit, but the girl has a pet dog.
My bike is small but fast.
I ride my bike to school every day, but I don't ride it on Sundays.
The train is fast, but the bus is slow.
We like reading, but they like watching TV.
A helicopter can fly, but it can't sail.
I can walk to the school, but my brother can't.
I am young, but my grandfather is old.
My scooter has two wheels, but our car has four wheels.
My dad takes the subway, but my mom takes the bus.
A ferry goes on water, but a helicopter goes in the air.

source.note: Workbook pages 34 and 38, and Grammar Workbook pages 12-13 used for contrast logic.
